reason: All 9 blocking checks (a)-(i) pass. Composite 97.1288 >= 85 gate; all 15 dimensions (linting, type_safety, test_coverage, security, secrets_scanning, license_compliance, mutation_testing, architecture, readability, error_handling, documentation, performance, integration_coverage, test_assertion_quality, traceability) meet their thresholds; 8/8 FRs at 95.0 PASS; open_critical/high/medium/low all = 0; composite matches gate4_result.json harness output (97.1288); BASELINE.md and VERIFICATION_REPORT.md both cited for provenance; 15 dimensions match gate4_result.json sanity check; per-FR contribution to composite is consistent. Two low-severity gaps: QUALITY_REPORT.md table lacks a threshold column (thresholds only in gate4_result.json), and the 14 mutation survivors in ssml_parser._cardinal_to_chinese are mentioned but not explained in the report body (explanation lives in gate4_result.json findings). Neither is blocking.

gaps.message: QUALITY_REPORT.md assessment-dimensions table (L11-27) shows Score/Status/Detail but no Threshold column. Thresholds are only present in .methodology/gate4_result.json breakdown. A reader of the report alone cannot verify score-vs-threshold without cross-referencing gate4_result.json.

QUALITY_REPORT.md L19 (Mutation Testing) reports '14 survived (unique IDs)' but does not explain WHY they survived. The explanation ('string literal mutations in _cardinal_to_chinese, hard to kill with tests') is in gate4_result.json L64-72 findings array but not surfaced in the report body. The phrasing 'hard to kill' also does not strictly assert 'equivalent mutants' — it admits either equivalent-mutant or test-gap interpretation.
